diagrams.title: GLUT Menu Creation Flowchart
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step process of creating and attaching a GLUT menu
elements: Add submenus: create submenu first, then glutAddSubMenu("Label", submenuID)
glutAttachMenu(GLUT_RIGHT_BUTTON) to bind to mouse button
Menu selection invokes callback with value
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete lifecycle of GLUT menu creation and usage

diagrams.title: Hierarchical Menu Structure
diagrams.description: Example of a nested menu system with main menu and submenus
elements: Main Menu: "Draw" → (Submenu: Points, Lines, Triangles, Circles)
Main Menu: "Color" → (Submenu: Red, Green, Blue, Custom)
Each entry has associated integer value passed to callback
diagrams.purpose: Understand how to organize complex menu systems with cascading submenus

diagrams.title: Menu Callback Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how user interaction flows through callback mechanism
elements: User performs action (right-click) → GLUT detects event
GLUT displays menu at cursor position
User selects menu item → GLUT captures selection
GLUT invokes registered callback function with item's value
Callback function executes application logic using switch/value
Control returns to GLUT main loop
diagrams.purpose: Understand event-driven callback execution in menu systems
